answer:
**The poem "Goristan-e-Shahi" ("The Royal Graveyard") from Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara Part 3* (ch. 9) evocatively describes a desolate royal graveyard (Goristan-e-Shahi in Lahore) filled with the tombs of Mughal emperors and nobles, contemplating themes of imperial glory, decline, and mortality.**[^1]

However, there are **no explicit lines where Iqbal (the poetic speaker) describes himself walking or strolling among the royal tombs**. The poem opens with a vivid scene-setting in the graveyard under a cloudy sky:

> The Royal Graveyard[^2]  
> The sky, in clouds, an ancient mantle wears,  
> The mirror of the moon a sullied face now bears.[^3]

It proceeds as a meditative reflection on the tombs ("A graveyard for the tumults of the past"; "So wondrous is the glory of each tomb"), ruins, stars, history, and nature, but the speaker remains an observer rather than a physical wanderer.[^4] The perspective implies presence amid the site, yet no verbs like "walk," "stroll," or "amid" denote movement through the tombs.

If this refers to a specific translation or interpretation outside the Falsafa corpus (which uses the "thothica" English rendering), it is not present here.[^5]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 9: "Goristan-e-Shahi" ([full chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/)).
[^2]: [p-814248](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-814248).
[^3]: [p-5921aa](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-5921aa).
[^4]: Key tomb references include [p-2e4cf2](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-2e4cf2) ("Once filled with life, now desolate and vast, / A graveyard for the tumults of the past"), [p-49c46c](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-49c46c) ("A graveyard, yes, but dust of high degree"), and [p-8863f3](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-8863f3) ("So wondrous is the glory of each tomb").
[^5]: No matches for "walk(s)," "stroll," "amid tombs," or similar in the translation, transliteration, or original (confirmed via corpus search).
